what_it_does: Grounds the FRAME doc's lean organ-map ('~80% built') against the real ~/company code: a 13-row table where each Surface organ is mapped to a verified file:line:symbol with a status (✅ exists / 🟡 partial / 🔴 net-new), flags three FRAME rows that overclaimed, computes a built-percentage from the cites (~65%), and assembles a precise net-new build backlog of what the Surface needs that the engine lacks.
observations: Frames itself as the LANE-GROUND deliverable for composition ① (the repo-exocortex), converting an unverified lean reading into citeable evidence; read-only, cross-references FRAME-the-two-halves.md without editing it.

Documents a clean degradation: the space='repo' corpus was empty at run-time (list_corpus(project='company') → 0), so grounding was done by direct deterministic grep+read rather than corpus query — itself recorded as finding row 10 (the ① ingest has never run on the repo).

Includes a RE-VERIFIED note (2026-06-09 ~20:40) by a later beat confirming cites still resolve, corpus still empty, and that embed-bge is now resident (company gpu: 4.9GB co-resident with chat-4b) so net-new #8 is unblocked.
States the cross-cutting truth: the engine has the PRIMITIVES and PATTERNS; many specific Surface organs are net-new.

The 13-row table cites concrete symbols: run_items/run_reduce/SlotBudget (cognition.py), append_mark/marks_for (fs_store.py), mark/capture_corpus (suite.py), the wire (surface_build_intent/dispatch_decision/drive_dispatchable), and the FE regions (RhmChat.tsx etc.).

Three overclaim corrections: row 3 (no-confidence is convention not enforced — mark() accepts a confidence field via **fields), row 8 (no first-class Decision-Ledger/Assumption-Shelf/Missing-Info-Map objects, only adjacent inbox/governance/coa substrate), row 6 (lens pattern ✅ but §20.4 extractor roles 🔴).
Built-% computed transparently: ✅=1.0, 🟡=0.5, 🔴=0.0 over 13 rows → 8.5/13 = ~65%, versus the FRAME's lean ~80%.

Net-new backlog: the schema-rendered generative surface (the hard 🔴), §20.4 extractor roles, ⑨ durable cross-session memory, progressive-type-resolution wiring, schema→UI render, first-class ledger/shelf/map objects, optional no-confidence enforcement, plus the ACTION of running the ① repo ingest.

A VERIFY-BY-USE section lists the exact grep/Read checks that confirmed each load-bearing claim (mark() confidence param, missing session_memo, no generative-surface render, empty corpus, the spine symbols).
Explicitly resists 'green-paint': refuses to downgrade row 13 to 🟡 just because FE regions exist, since those are the extension base, not the generative surface.
standouts: Built-% is derived from per-row cites with a stated weighting rather than guessed, converting a lean estimate into a defensible 65%.
The empty-corpus finding becomes a first-class deliverable: row 10's code exists but the ① ingest is a net-new ACTION never run.
Disciplined overclaim-flagging with grep evidence (e.g. mark() accepting confidence makes no-confidence a convention, not a law).
Anti-green-paint stance: distinguishes 'extension base' from 'partial build' so existing FE regions don't earn false credit.
conventions: Strict evidence discipline: every status resolves to a real file:line:symbol, with a degrade-clean note when corpus query was unavailable.
Uses the company's circled-composition vocabulary (①②③⑨⑩⑫), the ✅/🟡/🔴 legend, and §-section references to the Surface doc.
Read-only grounding deliverable that cross-references but never edits the source FRAME doc; provenance noted per beat (authored vs re-verified).
concerns: Status snapshot tied to a specific run-time (2026-06-09); the corpus-empty and embedder-resident facts will drift as the ingest runs and services change.
The 65% figure depends on the chosen 1.0/0.5/0.0 weighting and the 13-row partition, which are reasonable but somewhat arbitrary.
notes: A grounded, citeable audit that re-scores the FRAME organ-map from ~80% to ~65% built, flags three overclaims, and hands Tim a precise net-new backlog (headed by the schema-rendered generative surface) plus the un-run repo ingest action.
questions: Has the ① repo-exocortex ingest been run since this doc (is the repo space now populated)?
Did Tim decide whether no-confidence should become an enforced schema invariant or stay a convention?
Which existing memory substrate (episodic plugin vs file-memory) does ⑨ durable memory extend rather than parallel?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — it declares itself the grounded verification of the FRAME organ-map and delivers cited statuses, overclaim flags, a computed built-%, and a net-new backlog exactly as stated.
apparent_themes: Primitives/patterns exist vs specific organs are net-new
The product face (generative surface) as the headline gap
Clean degradation when a preferred data source is unavailable
intention_signals: Trying to give Tim a trustworthy, citeable map of what is actually built versus claimed, so the build backlog is real and steerable rather than inflated by a lean reading or partial-build credit.
novelty: Computing a built-percentage from per-row file:symbol cites with an explicit weighting, and elevating an empty-corpus observation into a named net-new action distinct from net-new code.
contribution: Contributes the grounded organ-map itself: the 13-row verified status table with real symbol cites, the three documented overclaim corrections, the derived ~65% built figure, and the precise eight-item net-new backlog the Surface still needs.
summary_for_embedding: A grounded audit of the FRAME organ-map verifying each Surface organ against real ~/company code with file:symbol cites, correcting three overclaims, computing ~65% built, and listing the precise net-new backlog.
intention_for_embedding: To replace a lean ~80%-built estimate with an evidence-grounded map of what is actually built versus net-new, giving Tim a citeable backlog to steer the Surface build.
